items.title: 2026 Federal Utility Partnership Working Group (FUPWG) Seminar
items.content_text: The Federal Utility Partnership Working Group (FUPWG) Seminar meets to share best practices, success stories, case studies, information about Federal Energy Management Program (FEMP) activities, and other business.

items.title: 2026 Electromagnetic Transient (EMT) Simulation Workshop
items.content_text: NERC, in collaboration with OE and ORNL, will host the 2026 Electromagnetic Transient (EMT) Simulation Workshop on Aug. 18-21, 2026, in Washington, D.C. Researchers, consultants, utilities, system operators, and reliability organizations will explore advancements in EMT simulations, including modeling, simulation techniques, input data structure, and automation tools to support evolving industry needs. This workshop will also feature hands-on tutorials on EMT simulation that is supported by DOE i2x.

items.title: FERC will impose reforms if PJM fails to adopt changes by September, chairman warns
items.content_text: There appears to be broad support for strengthening the independence of the grid operator’s board, possibly by moving to an advisory stakeholder process, and giving states more influence. Some also called for codifying the public interest in PJM’s mission.

items.title: Renewables, storage can meet one-third of US industrial heat demand: report
items.content_text: The UC Berkeley analysis should be welcome news for a manufacturing industry that is heavily reliant on natural gas, with its volatile prices, said Jose Dominguez, one of the report’s authors.

items.title: GE Vernova gas turbine backlog climbs to 116 GW
items.content_text: GE Vernova is taking reservations for 2031 deliveries now, said CEO Scott Strazik. He spoke of an increasingly diverse customer base, including data centers.

items.title: People are critical infrastructure, too. Utilities must do more or lose talent to tech.
items.content_text: With so much riding on America's AI ambitions, utility workforce strategy must have a place alongside generation, transmission and capital planning, writes Stefan Hirniak, president of Specialist Staffing Group US.

items.title: DOE’s new power export rule at odds with ‘energy emergency’ findings: Public Citizen
items.content_text: “Any export of power ... necessarily makes that power unavailable to domestic customers,” potentially contributing to inadequate supplies for a region, Public Citizen said.

items.title: FERC eyes ‘grid-enhancing technology’ incentives: Chairman Swett
items.content_text: Data centers, competition in the transmission sector and PJM Interconnection governance reform were other key topics at the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee hearing.

items.title: Ford, Global Power Products debut home backup solution
items.content_text: More than 800 electric utilities have approved GPP’s GenerLink transfer switch, which provides backup power for outlets and hardwired appliances at significantly lower cost than a standby generator, Ford says.

items.title: Don’t gut the utility model just as we need it most
items.content_text: Cutting utility returns won't lower electric bills and will leave us with a more fragile power grid, writes Scott Aaronson, former secretary of the Electricity Subsector Coordinating Council.

items.title: Wisconsin gas plant proposals test state review process
items.content_text: Integrated resource planning would give regulators a clearer long-term picture of electricity needs before major energy investments are approved, advocates say.

items.title: SRP to propose up to 1.7-GW mixed-resource project to its board in September
items.content_text: The Marigold Energy Center, about 45 miles south of Phoenix, would include 400 MW of battery storage, 600 MW of solar generation, up to 675 MW of gas generation and a new substation.
